// @generated — labelle-assembler null backend
// No window, no input, no audio, no renderer interaction. The main loop
// runs for a fixed number of frames (LABELLE_NULL_FRAMES env var, default
// 5) at a constant 1/60s timestep, then exits cleanly so `defer`-bound
// teardown (PluginControllers.deinit, GPA leak check) actually runs.
const screen_w: u32 = {{width}};
const screen_h: u32 = {{height}};
const screen_title = "{{title}}";
const target_fps: u32 = {{fps}};

const DEFAULT_NULL_FRAMES: u32 = 5;

/// Read LABELLE_NULL_FRAMES from the environment, falling back to
/// DEFAULT_NULL_FRAMES when unset or unparseable. Bounded so a typo
/// (`LABELLE_NULL_FRAMES=infinity`) never deadlocks CI.
fn getMaxFrames(allocator: std.mem.Allocator) u32 {
    const raw = std.process.getEnvVarOwned(allocator, "LABELLE_NULL_FRAMES") catch return DEFAULT_NULL_FRAMES;
    defer allocator.free(raw);
    return std.fmt.parseInt(u32, raw, 10) catch DEFAULT_NULL_FRAMES;
}

pub fn main() !void {
    var gpa = std.heap.GeneralPurposeAllocator(.{}){};
    defer _ = gpa.deinit();
    const allocator = gpa.allocator();

{{hooks_init_block}}
    var g = AssembledGame.init(allocator);
    defer g.deinit();

    g.setHooks(&hooks);

    g.setScreenHeight(@as(f32, @floatFromInt(screen_h)));
    g.getCamera().setPosition(0, @as(f32, @floatFromInt(screen_h)));

{{setup_code}}
    const max_frames = getMaxFrames(allocator);
    const dt: f32 = 1.0 / 60.0;
    var frame: u32 = 0;
    while (frame < max_frames) : (frame += 1) {
{{tick_code}}        g.tick(dt);
{{gui_draw_code}}    }
}
